persona_prompt:
You are filtering Boston events for Chloe, who wants upscale, interesting things to do in Boston.
She loves:
- Art: gallery openings, museum events, art shows, artist talks, art fairs, photography exhibits, sculpture
- Food & drink: wine tastings, wine dinners, chef's table experiences, cocktail classes, food festivals, upscale restaurant events, mixology
- Fancy experiences: galas, benefit dinners, charity auctions, cultural receptions, film premieres, theater openings
- Design, fashion, and aesthetics — runway shows, trunk shows, luxury boutique events, bridal showcases, couture
- Wellness and beauty events that are upscale (spa launches, product launches, brand events)
- Sip-and-craft events (sip & paint, pottery, floral arranging) — she likes these IF the crowd seems upscale or scene-y; skip if it reads as basic/suburban
- Anything cultured, beautiful, or scene-y
- Asian cultural events are great (she is Chinese and appreciates these)

SCORE 9-10 (must attend):
- "Fashionably LATE" Thursdays at The Liberty Hotel — weekly interactive runway with models weaving through the crowd, free admission, scene-y crowd
- Boston Fashion Week (Oct 2026) events, especially luxury eveningwear, couture, and accessory showcases
- Boston En Vogue — luxury gown and couture runway shows
- Trunk shows at luxury boutiques (Galia Lahav bridal, Agent Provocateur, Newbury St boutiques)
- SwimBoston — annual swimwear/bikini runway at Boston Harbor Distillery
- Any event at a Newbury St boutique, Back Bay gallery, or luxury hotel
- Museum opening receptions with cocktails or wine — Harvard Art Museums, MFA, ICA, Gardner — the "art party" type evening where the crowd is cultured and interesting, not just a museum visit
- University arts/cultural events at Harvard, MIT, BU that are open to the public and have a reception component

Lower priority for her:
- Pure civic/government/policy events (unless they're glamorous)
- Sports
- Basic networking mixers with no food/drink/art angle
- Sip-and-craft events at chain venues or with a generic/tourist vibe (score ≤ 4)

events.name: Fashionably Late Thursdays - 2026 Winter/Spring Season
events.description: Fashionably Late brings Boston's fashion scene to life.  Every Thursday, the hotel's lobby transforms into an interactive fashion show.
events.venue: Liberty Lobby at The Liberty Hotel
events.reason: Fashionably LATE Thursdays at Liberty Hotel — perfect match; weekly interactive runway, models, scene-y crowd, fashion-forward, free admission, weekly opportunity.

events.name: Fazendo a América: Rosângela Rennó and Histories of Memory and Migration in Brazil
events.description: First solo show in a U.S. museum in nearly 30 years featuring a two-part project by Brazilian artist Rosângela Rennó, including 48 portraits of Brazilian immigrants and a bilingual newspaper. The exhibition highlights the impact of the Brazilian community on the U.S., with over half the portraits depicting Brazilians living in Greater Boston.
events.venue: Museum of Fine Arts, Boston - Linde Family Wing for Contemporary Art
events.reason: Art exhibition opening (Fazendo a América, Rosângela Rennó at museum) — major solo show with cultural significance, Brazilian artist, immigrant narratives. Likely to have opening reception. Strong fit for Chloe's art interests and cultural appreciation.
